title: salix viminalis subsp. veriviminalis
intro: Salix viminalis subsp. veriviminalis, also known as the common osier or European osier, is a subspecies of the Salix viminalis plant, which is a type of willow tree native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, derived from its bark and leaves. The medicinal properties of Salix viminalis subsp. veriviminalis have been used to treat various health issues, including pain, fever, and rheumatism, due to its salicylic acid content. Salix viminalis subsp. veriviminalis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
